articleSummary: The article argues that AngularJS v2.0’s mandatory shift to TypeScript creates a steep onboarding barrier for developers, drawing a direct parallel to the disruptive Python 2-to-3 transition. While acknowledging TypeScript’s technical merits alongside ES6 and TC-39 standards, the author warns that newcomers must conquer “Mount TypeScript” before effectively building Angular applications. Written in a conversational, slightly critical yet cautiously optimistic tone, the piece targets JavaScript developers and framework evaluators navigating modern web tooling. Recurring metaphors like “Mount TypeScript” and GIF-based framing devices underscore the article’s central concern about developer overwhelm and onboarding friction.
